tools.name: get_ticket
tools.description: Pull the full ticket record including subject, body, submitter email, and timestamps. Call this first to understand the issue.

tools.name: lookup_customer_by_email
tools.description: Find the customer account from an email address. Use the submitter email from the ticket to get the customer_id you need for all other tools.

email.description: Email address of the ticket submitter

tools.name: get_billing_history
tools.description: Retrieve the last 12 months of invoices, payments, plan changes, and credits for a customer. Use this to verify charges, confirm plan change dates, and find transaction IDs needed for refunds.

tools.name: check_plan_entitlements
tools.description: Return what features and usage limits the customer's current plan includes (e.g. seat cap, API call limit, storage). Use before confirming whether a charge or feature is expected.

tools.name: issue_refund
tools.description: Refund a specific charge back to the customer's payment method. Use when the transaction_id is known from billing history. For corrections to an open or future invoice instead, use adjust_invoice.

transaction_id.description: Transaction ID from get_billing_history, e.g. txn_abc123

amount.description: Amount to refund in USD. Must be greater than 0 and not exceed the original transaction amount.

reason.description: duplicate_charge = same amount charged twice. billing_error = wrong amount or wrong plan billed. service_credit = compensating for a service failure. goodwill = discretionary credit.

tools.name: adjust_invoice
tools.description: Apply a credit or correction to an open invoice. Use when the invoice_id is known and the invoice is not yet paid. For reversing a charge that has already been processed, use issue_refund instead.

invoice_id.description: Invoice ID from get_billing_history, e.g. inv_abc123

credit_description.description: Reason for the credit, e.g. 'Plan downgrade correction — Scale to Growth Feb 26'

tools.name: check_payment_method
tools.description: Return the payment method on file: card type, last 4 digits, expiry, and most recent failure if any. Use when investigating payment failures or declined charges.
